task_completion.description: Set the correct historical date, time, and location in Stellarium.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Stellarium Date/Time window or bottom toolbar showing the date '1912-04-15' and time '02:20' (or very close to it). The location window or status bar should indicate coordinates near 41.726°N, 50.233°W.

task_completion.description: Configure the sky display settings according to the task requirements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium sky view must show NO ground/landscape (stars should be visible below the horizon line), constellation lines must be drawn between stars, constellation names/labels must be visible, and the atmosphere toggle should be enabled (often visible in the bottom toolbar).

task_completion.description: Locate and center the Moon in the view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the Stellarium interface with the Moon selected and centered in the middle of the screen.

task_completion.description: Locate and center Polaris in the view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the Stellarium interface with the star Polaris selected and centered in the middle of the screen.

task_completion.description: Locate and center Jupiter in the view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the Stellarium interface with the planet Jupiter selected and centered in the middle of the screen.

task_completion.description: Record research findings in the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, mousepad, or nano) open with the file '/home/ga/Desktop/titanic_sky_research.txt'. The text must explicitly contain the year '1912' and mention the 'moon' (e.g., noting whether it was visible or not).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the actual Stellarium application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots must show the distinct Stellarium graphical user interface (toolbars, search windows, specific rendering style), not generic images of the night sky downloaded from the internet.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the application GUI to find celestial bodies.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for open Stellarium dialog boxes, such as the Search window (F3) or Date/Time window (F5), indicating manual or GUI-driven navigation rather than purely backend script manipulation.

integrity.description: Verify the text file was created and edited properly.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file should be visible in a standard text editor or terminal output, showing genuine typing or file saving actions, rather than just a terminal command echoing text without verification.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target location is 41.726°N, 50.233°W. The target date is 1912-04-15. The required display settings are: landscape disabled (ground off), atmosphere enabled, constellation lines enabled, and constellation labels enabled. The agent must capture at least 3 screenshots (Moon, Polaris, Jupiter) saved to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/. The research notes must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/titanic_sky_research.txt and include the year '1912' and whether the 'moon' was visible.
